title: Rhodanthemum pseudocatananche
taxonomy: Rhodanthemum pseudocatananche belongs to the family Asteraceae. It is a species of flowering plant. Rhodanthemum pseudocatananche is native to the Mediterranean region. The plant is often classified as an annual or biennial herb. It is commonly found in dry, rocky areas and open woodlands.

intro: Rhodanthemum pseudocatananche, also known as False Catananche, is a perennial herbaceous plant in the Asteraceae family, characterized by its bright yellow flowers and pungent, camphor-like fragrance. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help alleviate respiratory issues and skin conditions. Bioactive constituents of Rhodanthemum pseudocatananche include camphor, borneol, and sesquiterpenes, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used in herbal preparations such as essential oils, tinctures, and infusions, often employed to relieve respiratory congestion, coughs, and skin irritations.
